$Comment: Smoke round R5 (2026-07-22): Custody.Display.Offset.Y lowered from 0.62 to 0.05 so the placed log rests on the bench surface instead of floating - a maintainer-tunable FIRST GUESS from this round, not yet re-verified in-game. Round-4 re-tune (2026-07-22): Custody.Display.Offset.Y lowered further, from 0.05 to -0.1 (maintainer-chosen) - verified against Custody.Display.Offset's codec (asset.Custody.Display.Offset.CODEC, plain Codec.DOUBLE leaves, no clamp) and StationCustodyDisplay#resolvePosition (blockY+0.5+offset.getY(), a bare addition, no clamp) before authoring: a negative Y legally decodes and legally embeds the placed log slightly below the block-top anchor. Identity.Icon removed: StationService#blockItemIdAt already falls back to the anchor block's own BlockType id when Identity.Icon is absent/blank, and that id equals this station's own item id (RPG_Station_Sawmill, filename = item id per repo convention) - so the summary-HUD crest (StationSummaryHud#renderStationIcon) already shows the station's own block icon with zero duplicated id to drift. R7 fix-round correction (2026-07-22): that claim was FALSE at engage time - a Loaded custody block is a distinct generated-key BlockType (*RPG_Station_Sawmill_Loaded, per StateData#generateBlockKey), so the old getId()-only fallback resolved the UNKNOWN placeholder icon, not this station's own. Fixed engine-side: StationService#blockItemIdAt now resolves via BlockType#getItem() (the block's containing Item asset), which walks correctly through a state variant back to the base RPG_Station_Sawmill item id - the fallback's original zero-duplicated-id-to-drift intent now genuinely holds. See RPG Stations' station/CLAUDE.md for the full source trail; no authoring change needed here. Round-4 puppet-presentation content leg (2026-07-22): authored a station-level Puppet group (Enabled true, Hide.Route Scale, the maintainer-crowned in-game-proven default; Look.Source PlayerClone, the maintainer's literal 'their character model'; Prop.Source MirrorHeld so the puppet carries a copy of whatever hatchet the player holds) so the real player is scale-hidden while a cloned puppet works the bench in their place. Offset/Yaw are a first-pass placement guess (world-space off the block-top anchor, not compensated for the block's own placement rotation, kept small per Custody.Display's own documented caveat) pending the P7 in-game smoke round; every leaf here is maintainer-tunable. Puppet fix round (2026-07-22): the maintainer's in-game smoke of that first authored use found the puppet facing the WRONG way and standing too close to the bench. Verified against code before re-authoring (this leg's own resolution-semantics check, RPG Stations' StationPuppetController#spawnAndHide + ziggfreed-common's PlayerPuppetService#offsetPosition/#spawn): Offset/Yaw resolve EXACTLY as authored, a plain world-space anchor-plus-offset addition and a direct Rotation3f(0, yawRadians, 0) applied to the spawn holder's TransformComponent with NO engine-side rotation on this path - the BlockMountPoint hard +180 quirk documented on this same pack's RPG_Station_Sawmill.json item asset applies ONLY to the Seat-mount route's BlockType.Seats[].Yaw (native BlockMountAPI.mountOnBlock), a completely different codec and code path the puppet's raw commandBuffer.addEntity spawn never touches. So the wrong facing/distance is a pure content miss, not an engine bug. Fixed by content re-tune: Yaw rotated 180 from the prior guess (180.0 -> 0.0) and Offset.Z stepped back from 0.4 to 0.7 (maintainer-tunable first guesses again, pending re-confirm). The anvil's own Puppet group is untouched this round (not yet independently smoke-tested).
